subject: Re: Warp GUI to Warptool
body:
Thanks for outlining this Rasmus! I added a docs page containing your nice
outline here
https://warpem.github.io/warp/reference/warptools/processing_warp_v1_data/

Cheers,

Alister

On Mon, 17 Mar 2025 at 08:44, Rasmus Jensen <rasmuskjeldsenj@gmail.com>
wrote:

>
> I've converted several datasets from Warp1.0.X to Warp2.0.0 and for me it
> works quite well.
>
> I normally copy (symbolically link) the averaged frames (after motion
> correction) and run frame series CTF estimation again. I then copy over the
> masks from Warp1.0.X boxnet. Finally, I import my previous .xf files, and
> run tilt series CTF estimation.
>
> After exporting particles from star files generated in RELION from
> Warp1.0.X I can just reconstruct and get similar looking maps without
> re-refining angles etc.
>
> As Reika mentioned make sure you have the same handedness and also
> dimensions of your tomograms.
> mandag den 17. februar 2025 kl. 20.29.34 UTC+1 skrev alist...@gmail.com:
>
>> Hey Dip,
>>
>> Apologies but 1 -> 2 was a major version change and as such we don't
>> guarantee backwards compatibility.
>>
>> Your best bet for minimal headaches is to reprocess, import existing tilt
>> series alignments, reconstruct in WarpTools and repick. If you absolutely
>> need to reuse your picks you will need to carefully compare tomograms and
>> will likely run into a bunch of annoying issues getting the tomograms to
>> match exactly.
>>
>> I understand how much work repicking can be if you go that route.
>>
>> Cheers,
>>
>> Alister
>>
>> On Mon, 17 Feb 2025 at 08:38, Souradip Mukherjee <
>> souradip.mu...@gmail.com> wrote:
>>
>>> Has anyone tried moving their data processing from Warp GUI to warptools
>>> at a later stage of processing? I was processing my data through Warp GUI
>>> before and recently we bought a new linux machine where we installed Warp2.
>>> I have picked particles, I just want to export the subtomograms from Warp
>>> using the command line. I realise that it needs a tilt series settings file
>>> but I cannot seem to find it anywhere in my working directory. Do I have to
>>> start back from preprocessing once again or is there a way to create the
>>> right directory structure and settings file? Can anyone suggest anything
>>> please?
>>>
>>> Dip
>>>
>>> Souradip Mukherjee
>>> Centriole Architecture Lab
>>> University of Geneva
>>>
>>> --

>>> Groups "Warp" group.
